title: feat: support AI-generated masks in shape_to_mask
body:
Updated the export functionality to support AI-generated masks. The function now handles mask data from JSON files, including AI-generated mask formats (list/array and base64 encoded images).

What this implies:
- Shape-to-mask generation should be able to consume an already-provided mask (from JSON) in addition to producing a mask from shape geometry.
- AI-generated masks can be provided as a Python/list/array, or as a base64-encoded image, and must be correctly aligned to the target image/shape coordinates.
- Backward compatibility: If no mask is provided in JSON, existing behavior (rendering shapes to masks) should remain intact.

Proposed integration points (based on the codebase):
- labelme/utils/shape.py: Extend the core function shape_to_mask to accept an optional mask parameter. If present, normalize/convert the provided mask (list/array or base64-encoded image) to a boolean mask of the target image size. If not present, fall back to the current polygon/shape-based rendering path.
- labelme/cli/export_json.py: Ensure the export path supports shapes that carry AI-generated masks. If shapes contain a mask field (list/array or base64), pass it through to shape_to_mask; otherwise retain the existing rendering path via shapes_to_label.
- labelme/utils/shape.py (or related deprecation wrappers like polygons_to_mask): Update to handle the new mask input formats and maintain backward compatibility for existing pipelines.

Notes:
- The current export path in export_json.py uses utils.shapes_to_label to build a label mask from shapes. With AI-generated masks, shapes_to_label should be able to utilize shape_to_mask with an externally supplied mask, instead of always rendering from the geometric points.
- If the AI-generated mask is provided as a base64-encoded image, the code must decode and threshold/convert to a boolean mask of the same dimensions as the image being annotated.
- Thorough validation is needed to ensure masks align with the image and shape coordinates when overlaid or exported as label maps.

step_plan.summary: This PR adds AI-generated mask support to shape_to_mask by accepting JSON-provided masks, updates the export path to propagate these masks, and preserves backward-compatible behavior when no AI mask is provided.

steps.operation: Extend shape_to_mask to accept an optional mask parameter and normalize provided AI-generated mask to a boolean mask of the target image size. If a mask is not provided, preserve the existing shape-based rendering path.
steps.reason: Enable consuming AI-generated masks from JSON (list/array or base64) and align them with the target image/shape coordinates while preserving backward compatibility.
steps.side_effects: Potential changes to function signature; downstream callers must handle optional mask parameter; unit tests must cover new mask formats; may introduce dependencies on numpy/Pillow; ensure compatibility with existing code paths.

steps.operation: Update export_json CLI path to pass through AI-generated masks from JSON shapes to the updated shape_to_mask; otherwise fall back to existing shapes_to_label pipeline.
steps.reason: To ensure the export flow uses AI-generated masks when provided in shapes, while keeping current behavior when masks are absent.
steps.side_effects: Backward compatibility maintained for non-masked shapes; need tests for export with AI masks; potential minor changes in label map outputs.

steps.operation: Update or wrap polygons_to_mask to support the new mask input formats or delegate to the updated shape_to_mask when a mask is provided.
steps.reason: Maintain backward compatibility and centralize mask handling in shape_to_mask by routing to updated logic when AI mask data exists.
steps.side_effects: Tests and external dependencies referencing polygons_to_mask may need updates; ensure no regressions in exported label maps; potential duplication of logic.
